tldr: Went through all 23 live ads and the biggest issue isn't concept count — you're at 9 distinct angles, which clears Andromeda's floor — it's that roughly half your ads are shipping with unrendered template tags where the headline and body should be, which reads as 'product name' and 'product brand' literally to a scrolling parent. On top of that, four of your bib ads have been running 27–29 days on the same 'Magnetic silicone bib' headline, right at the edge of your 21-day refresh window.

benchmark.context: Your practical refresh cadence is landing around 18 days once you account for the four bib ads that have crossed day 27. The category median for brands at your scale is ~21 days and the top quartile is closer to 14 — you're between the two, but the broken template ads are burning impressions that should be doing work.

ads.headline: {{product.name}}
drivers: Both the headline and body are literally unrendered template code — a parent scrolling sees '{{product.name}}' where a benefit should be, which kills the click before it starts
27 days running past your 21-day window with zero copy doing any lifting
Landing on the homepage instead of a product or collection page, so even a curious tap has nowhere focused to go

ads.headline: {{product.name}}
drivers: Same broken-template pattern — no headline and no body copy is actually rendering, the ad is running on image alone
20 days in, right at your refresh threshold with nothing text-side to keep it fresh
Homepage landing means the ad has no product context to anchor the click

ads.headline: Magnetic Silicone Bib
drivers: No body copy on this ad — the unrendered brand tag is doing all the work below the image, which means the primary text slot is empty for a parent skimming feed
28 days running with a headline that just names the category ('Magnetic Silicone Bib') rather than earning the click
This exact headline pattern is repeated on four other live ads — Andromeda is clustering these together

ads.headline: Magnetic silicone bib
drivers: Body copy is the unrendered template tag — no story, no benefit, no proof anywhere on the ad
29 days running, eight days past your typical refresh window
The category-name-as-headline approach is now duplicated across at least four active ads pointing at the same collection

ads.headline: Magnetic silicone bib
ads.body: "Necessity is the mother of invention" ⚡️ Easy to snap on. Tough for little ones to pull off. Designed by parents who know mealtime.
drivers: 29 days on the same 'Necessity is the mother of invention' hook — the copy itself is strong but it's been on the same clustered creative for a month
This exact quote is recycled verbatim as the headline on a separate ad, so Meta is reading two of your creatives as near-identical
Landing on a single product page while the headline names the whole category creates a mismatch

ads_compact.headline: Mealtime just got a whole lot easier.
Magnetic silicone bib
ads_compact.body: And just like that, you have a favorite bib 🧲⚡️🤍

next_step.urgency: Four bib ads cross day 30 this week — and eight more are running with broken template tags right now.
next_step.headline: Walk through the Babynetic refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Friend, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep — work with us or don't.

method_note: Went through all 23 live ads in your Meta Ad Library, mapped copy duplication, headline patterns, days-running, and format mix. Public data only — no account access, no CPM/ROAS claims about your account.
